item.title: Mexico Bets on Supercomputer to Combat Extreme Weather Events
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum is working to develop climate models that will help prevent extreme weather events, part of a push to set up a public supercomputer that can use millions of data points to tackle national challenges.

item.title: Bank of France Trims Growth Forecast, Sees Faster Inflation
item.description: The Bank of France reduced its 2026 economic-growth forecast and lifted its inflation prediction as it includes the impact of surging energy prices caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Dolce & Gabbana Gears Up for Lender Talks as Debt Pressure Rises
item.description: Dolce & Gabbana is embarking on fresh talks with lenders after weak global demand for luxury goods put pressure on earnings and the terms governing its debt, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: AI Doomer’s New Warning: Market Is Wrong to Ditch Rate-Cut Bets
item.description: A little over a month after James van Geelen sent stocks into a tailspin with his dystopian account of a post-AI economic collapse, he’s wagering that another fear-fueled selloff — this time, in the bond market — has run too far.

item.title: BofA Starts Private Capital M&A Team to Focus on PE Exits
item.description: Buyout firms are sitting on a massive stable of unsold companies. They’re holding them longer and looking for creative ways to monetize them, as the old bootstrap-and-flip nature of the business evolves.

item.title: The Problem with Private Credit ‘Working as Designed’
item.description: Liquidity at the gates.

item.title: Wealthsimple Joins Prediction Markets Fray as Canada Opens Up
item.description: More financial firms are wading into prediction markets in Canada, working to get the green light from regulators to bring a popular betting product to traders.

item.title: Apollo’s Insurance Arm Vaults to Second-Biggest FHLB Borrower
item.description: Apollo Global Management Inc.’s insurance arm was the second-biggest borrower last year in the Federal Home Loan Bank system, a Depression-era program designed to shore up mortgage lending that has morphed into a go-to — and controversial — source of cheap financing for banks and other financial institutions.

item.title: Italy Said to Plan Cutting Growth Forecast to as Low as 0.5%
item.description: The Italian government is set to cut its economic growth forecast, reflecting a hit from the war in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Electronic Arts’ New Dollar Debt Rallies After $15 Billion Sale
item.description: Investors are bidding up Electronic Arts Inc. dollar-denominated debt that priced on Tuesday to help fund the video game maker’s record buyout.

item.title: Meta, Google Found Liable in Social Media Addiction Case
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. and Alphabet Inc.’s Google were found liable for a 20-year-old woman who alleged that her addiction to the companies’ social media platforms caused her to suffer a mental health crisis, a jury said.

item.title: Musk’s xAI ‘Doubling Down’ on AI Videos After OpenAI Nixes Sora
item.description: Elon Musk said his artificial intelligence company, xAI, plans to upgrade its AI video generator, seizing on an opening left by rival OpenAI’s decision this week to discontinue its Sora product.

item.title: The Once-Sober ETF Industry Finds a Home on a Vegas Casino Floor
item.description: At 5:30 a.m. at the Virgin Hotels Las Vegas, the ETF industry’s biggest annual conference was taking place on the casino floor.

item.title: KKR-Backed OHB Taps Banks for Share Sale Amid Space Boom
item.description: OHB SE is working on a sale of shares in the German satellite maker, people familiar with the matter said, potentially allowing minority investor KKR & Co. to trim its stake.

item.title: Trump EPA Allows Summer Sales of Higher-Ethanol E15 Gasoline
item.description: The Trump administration will waive higher-ethanol E15 gasoline from US volatility requirements this summer, expanding sales and delivering a win for corn farmers and biofuels producers.

item.title: Goldman M&A Head Sees ‘Massive’ Capital Pools Driving M&A
item.description: Wall Street is awash in “massive pools of capital” that should drive M&A activity over the long-term, even as volatility upends dealmaking, according to one of Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s top dealmakers.

item.title: Ecopetrol Board to Decide on CEO Roa’s Future Amid Split
item.description: Ecopetrol SA’s board will meet March 30 to decide the fate of Chief Executive Officer Ricardo Roa, as pressure from politicians and workers risks paralyzing Colombia’s biggest company.

item.title: British Airways Proposes Bonus for Pilots Who Burn Less Fuel
item.description: British Airways is offering pilots a financial incentive to cut their aircraft’s fuel consumption, highlighting how carriers are trying to reduce one of their biggest expenses at a time of soaring oil prices and stricter climate regulations.

item.title: As US Strikes Hit Iran, AI Warfare is Debated At Washington Forum
item.description: Nearly 10,000 targets hit in Iran so far.

item.title: US Recession Risks Begin to Rise as War Dims Economic Outlook
item.description: Wall Street is cutting its forecasts for the US economy this year, boosting its projections for inflation and unemployment and nudging up the odds of a recession as the impact of the Iran war starts to come into view.

item.title: Software Firm Planview Pitches Private Credit to Refinance Debt
item.description: Software firm Planview Inc. has reached out to private credit firms to help it address debt maturing next year, according to people familiar with the situation.

item.title: Sony Announces New TV and Soundbars as TCL Deal Looms
item.description: Sony Group Corp. introduced a new midrange 4K TV and several home theater audio products, going about business as usual even as it nears a deal to sell a majority stake in its home entertainment business to China’s TCL Electronics Holdings Ltd.

item.title: Mozambique Dollar Bond Selloff Extends as Oil Price Shock Deepens Crisis
item.description: A selloff on Mozambique’s dollar bonds extended into a 10th day, as the oil shock from the Iran war deepens the country’s financial crisis.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Finds Less Treasuries Liquidity on War Volatility
item.description: Interest-rate strategists at Morgan Stanley say this month’s Treasury market slump has hallmarks of forced selling of two-year notes, whose yields soared as traders abandoned wagers on Federal Reserve interest-rate cuts and began to price in a hike.

item.title: Musk Deepens Feud With Delaware Judge, Alleging Bias on LinkedIn
item.description: Elon Musk’s lawyers are urging the Delaware Chancery Court’s chief judge to step aside from lawsuits involving him and his companies, alleging she has shown bias against the billionaire after ruling against him in high-profile cases.

item.title: IEA Chief to Brief EU Finance Ministers on War Impact This Week
item.description: The head of the International Energy Agency will participate in a meeting of European Union finance ministers this week as they craft possible responses to the war in the Middle East.

item.title: ‘Insane’ Volatility Hurts Energy Production Planning, Fed Survey Shows
item.description: Volatility in energy commodity prices driven by the ongoing war in the Middle East is confounding firms trying to make decisions about production in coming months.

item.title: SpaceX Said to Target as Much as $75 Billion in Blockbuster IPO
item.description: SpaceX is considering a fundraising target in its IPO that would dwarf the previous largest ever debut, according to people familiar with the matter, as billionaire Elon Musk’s rocket and satellite maker moves forward with listing plans.

item.title: Air Canada CEO Rebuked by Carney Over English Video on Crash
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney reprimanded Air Canada’s head for issuing a video statement in English only following the tragic runway collision at LaGuardia Airport in New York that killed two pilots.

item.title: UK’s Renewables Output Hits Record, Helping to Blunt Iran Shock
item.description: The UK’s renewables power output hit a record on Wednesday, with a surge in wind and solar generation helping to blunt the impact of the Middle East war on power prices.
